message:
test(protocol): live-oracle RESP2 round-trip gate — first increment (br-frankenredis-0zyf)

First incremental step toward the full fr-protocol conformance harness
described in the bead. Adds crates/fr-protocol/tests/live_oracle_diff.rs
with two tests that exercise the cross-impl round-trip contract from
the /testing-conformance-harnesses Pattern 3 playbook:

  1. live_oracle_roundtrip_canonical_corpus: spawns the vendored
     upstream redis-server out of legacy_redis_code/redis/src, sends
     28 canonical commands spanning every RESP2 reply shape (simple
     string, error, integer, bulk, null bulk, array, empty array,
     nil-array via LPOP-missing, nested array via HGETALL/ZRANGE), and
     for each raw reply asserts:
       - parse_frame(bytes) succeeds
       - parsed.consumed == bytes.len() (no trailing, no partial)
       - parsed.frame.to_bytes() == bytes (byte-for-byte re-encode)
       - reparse(reencode) == initial parse (structural identity)

  2. live_oracle_byte_exact_frames_for_canonical_replies: spot-pins the
     wire shape of a handful of well-known replies (PING -> +PONG\r\n,
     SET -> +OK\r\n, STRLEN -> :6\r\n, GET missing -> $-1\r\n, EXISTS
     missing -> :0\r\n, LRANGE missing -> *0\r\n) as byte-equal to our
     expectations, so any stray drift in parser or encoder breaks the
     gate at the narrowest possible surface.

The VendoredRedis spawner mirrors the pattern used in fr-conformance
(tests::VendoredRedis, br-frankenredis-5pnv) but is local to fr-protocol
because fr-protocol must not depend on fr-conformance (reverse-cycle
avoidance). Skipped silently when the binary is absent
(FR_CONFORMANCE_SKIP_LIVE_ORACLE=1 to force the skip).

Subsequent 0zyf increments will:
 - expand the corpus beyond 28 cases
 - add DISCREPANCIES.md / COVERAGE.md accounting per the skill
 - add RESP3 XFAIL fixtures (see frankenredis-joae goldens for pinned
   UnsupportedResp3Type markers already in place)
 - add a compliance-report generator

Co-Authored-By: cc-redis <noreply@anthropic.com>
